element_introduction_order.first_appears: Entry to Tournament / Forging of Warriors modes
element_introduction_order.mastery_context: Established from predecessor World Heroes 2; player assumed familiar with core mechanics

element_introduction_order.first_appears: Entry to Tournament mode - early tournament groups
element_introduction_order.mastery_context: New mobility option distinguishing this sequel; enables tactical spacing adjustments during matches

element_introduction_order.first_appears: Entry to Tournament mode - early group matches
element_introduction_order.mastery_context: Introduces risk/reward to aggressive spacing; requires timing adjustment vs. predecessors

element_introduction_order.element: Four tournament groups (3 opponents per group, must win 2 of 3)
element_introduction_order.first_appears: Entry to Tournament mode - initial phase
element_introduction_order.mastery_context: Mid-tier challenge: selection strategy and opponent familiarity

element_introduction_order.element: Captain Kidd as mandatory mid-boss (2 of 3 rounds required)
element_introduction_order.first_appears: Entry to Tournament mode - after initial 4 groups
element_introduction_order.mastery_context: Sustained difficulty spike; signals transition to boss encounters

element_introduction_order.element: Hanzou as secondary boss (2 of 3 rounds required)
element_introduction_order.first_appears: Entry to Tournament mode - after Captain Kidd
element_introduction_order.mastery_context: Second difficulty peak before final confrontation

element_introduction_order.element: Jack and Ryofu as dual mini-bosses (sequential battles)
element_introduction_order.first_appears: Entry to Tournament mode - final pre-Zeus gauntlet
element_introduction_order.mastery_context: Endurance test with back-to-back challenging opponents

element_introduction_order.first_appears: Entry to Tournament mode - climactic battle
element_introduction_order.mastery_context: Ultimate difficulty peak; represents narrative threat (Mr. Z's ultimate agent)

element_introduction_order.element: Forging of Warriors mode (single opponent selection, 4-win streak ending)
element_introduction_order.first_appears: Game menu - alt-mode after learning tournament structure
element_introduction_order.mastery_context: Arcade-style unlimited play format; emphasizes player choice and replayability vs. narrative progression

element_introduction_order.element: Stage selection tied to opponent choice
element_introduction_order.mastery_context: Environmental strategy layer; stage attributes influence matchup dynamics

typical_relief_triggers: Victory in 2-of-3 round format (completion of local match)
Returning to character select screen (breathing room between encounters)
Forging of Warriors mode vs. scripted tournament (player agency reduces tension)
typical_tension_triggers: Proceeding through tournament groups (increasing opponent quality expected)
Zeus final battle (narrative climax + mechanical difficulty)
Attack rebound system creating mid-match momentum shifts
rhythm_pattern.pacing_descriptor: Tournament structure creates wave-based tension: low-moderate in grouped matches, escalating through mid-bosses (Kidd, Hanzou), sustained through final gauntlet (Jack, Ryofu), and peaking at Zeus. Relief provided by round/group completion milestones. Forging of Warriors mode flattens pressure through player control.

difficulty_curve.early_game: Moderate: Tournament groups introduce core fighting against varied character types; dashing and rebound mechanics add complexity over predecessor
difficulty_curve.mid_game: High: Captain Kidd mid-boss presents significant skill wall; demands mastery of new movement options and attack timing
difficulty_curve.late_game: Very High: Hanzou, Jack, Ryofu, and Zeus create sustained pressure; multiple difficulty peaks without relief between encounters
notable_spikes: Captain Kidd transition (first mandatory 2-of-3 boss fight)
Hanzou encounter (second 2-of-3 boss requiring sustained play)
Zeus climax (final boss representing world domination threat narrative)

notable_level_patterns.description: Entry to Tournament mode structures difficulty through group-based advancement. Four groups of three opponents each (win 2 of 3 per group), followed by sequential boss encounters (Kidd, Hanzou, Jack, Ryofu, Zeus). Provides narrative throughline and graduated challenge progression.
notable_level_patterns.example_level: Entry to Tournament Mode - Complete Structure

notable_level_patterns.description: Alternative mode with player-selected opponent matching. Game ends after 4 consecutive wins. No narrative structure; emphasizes replayability and versus functionality. Stage selection derives from opponent's character stage.

Post-group tournament concludes with rapid-fire boss encounters: Captain Kidd (2-of-3), Hanzou (2-of-3), Jack (sequential), Ryofu (sequential), Zeus (final). Endurance test demanding sustained player performance across multiple difficulty peaks.
notable_level_patterns.example_level: Entry to Tournament - Final Boss Sequence

sources_used: wikipedia - World Heroes 2 Jet (Gameplay and Plot sections)
